what_it_does: This file is an implementation guide for building the operator surface: it lists cross-cutting principles, a sequenced plan of foundational bridge improvements, a fused PWA shell, and per-criterion surface builds, with file roles, do's/don'ts, and pseudocode.
observations: The guide is organized into cross-cutting principles, foundation improvements (A1-A5), the fused surface shell (B), per-surface builds (C-home through C-artefacts), a form/design rubric, and a preservation checklist.
It repeatedly instructs the surface to be a thin client of the bridge API and SSE stream, never a second event writer or FsStore.
It names specific files and line ranges to modify, and flags net-new bridge endpoints or static mounts.
It includes explicit safety/auth gating (B-auth) before tailnet exposure.
It marks S8 (comments), S9 (versions), and S10 (artefacts) as one coupled build because of a safe postMessage boundary change.
It uses an exact tense convention: IS = exists, SHOULD/WILL = planned.
standouts: 'Ride, don't re-derive' — the surface must consume /api and /api/stream only.
Honest partial coverage for channel filtering: don't claim channel-scoped stream until events that need channels actually carry them.
B-auth gates tailnet exposure until the existing /api/operator-session token is wired into consequential writes.
Comment-as-instruction: a 'do-this-now' flag routes to dispatch and threads the result back onto the comment.
S8+S9+S10 are explicitly coupled due to a security boundary change that breaks same-origin annotation injection.
concerns: A large number of net-new builds are flagged across surfaces, increasing integration surface area.
Tailnet exposure before auth is called out as dangerous, implying the current state may be unauthenticated.
S8/S9/S10 coupling suggests a big-bang dependency rather than incremental delivery.
Versioning is described as 'fully net-new' with real data-loss risk in current overwrite behavior.
The guide assumes familiarity with many external symbols (RHM, S15, tldraw-graph, DNA live-load) without defining them here.
notes: A prescriptive, sequenced build plan for a bridge-riding operator PWA with strong safety, design-system, and no-silent-failure constraints.
questions: What is the concrete directory name for the fused app?
What exactly is the RHM and how does /api/up-translate work?
What do the S1/S2/S4/etc. criterion numbers map to in detail?
What are 'tldraw-graph-as-surface' and 'DNA live-load' in this codebase?
How is the existing /api/operator-session token currently minted and validated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to explain how to make each completion criterion true, and the file does exactly that through principles, sequence, file roles, and do/don'ts.
apparent_themes: Thin-client riding a single bridge API
Safety and auth before network exposure
intention_signals: The file is trying to direct construction of a unified, human-centered operator interface by harvesting the right parts from two existing frontends, extending the bridge only where necessary, and keeping all consequential state and auth at the center.
novelty: It frames implementation as an interpretive ledger entry with explicit neutrality, refuses to crown a winner between the two existing apps, and marks coupled builds and honest partial coverage rather than pretending completeness.
contribution: It contributes a sequenced, file-role-level implementation plan and a set of cross-cutting constraints that a fused system would keep as the build checklist for the operator surface.
summary_for_embedding: An implementation guide that sequences foundational bridge improvements, a fused PWA shell, and per-criterion operator surfaces while enforcing thin-client API riding, design-system tokens, and no-silent-failure human notices.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a buildable, safety-first roadmap for constructing the operator surface from harvested frontend parts and additive bridge endpoints.
